title: Gate-1 e2e: train 2D U-Net on ACDC -> Dice + EF vs GT
description: Wire real ACDC Dataset into training/ on the processed cache. Patient-level split, train 2D U-Net, validate Dice per class, EF vs GT. Pipeline must run end-to-end with real numbers.

title: MRI: EF from predictions vs GT + failure analysis
description: EDV/ESV->EF, compare to GT; Hausdorff; rank_failures + why.

title: MRI theory: acquisition, short-axis geometry, EF foundations
description: Study writeups in learning/. Cardiac MRI cine, ED/ES, EF definition, anatomy, why intensity uncalibrated.

title: Gate 1: first public push (MRI Dice + EF vs GT + failure case)
description: Milestone: flip repo public when MRI lane has real numbers.
notes: e2e VERIFIED: 2D U-Net on ACDC (5090, torch cu130). VAL Dice LV-cav 0.927 / myo 0.814 / RV 0.843 (mean 0.861); EF MAE 4.5% over 20 val patients. Failure mode = HCM (thick wall). Baseline: 40ep, no aug.
close_reason: Gate-1 e2e done: reproducible 2D U-Net baseline Dice mean 0.871 (LV-cav 0.932), EF MAE 3.1%, failure analysis (RV/HCM). Pushed.

title: MRI: 2D U-Net seg (LV/myo/RV) on ACDC
description: Patient-level split. Track Dice per structure on val.

title: MRI data exploration (ACDC EDA)
description: Shapes, spacing spread, intensity dist, class balance, ED/ES; pick normalization + target spacing from data.

title: Acquire ACDC dataset
description: Register at Creatis/humanheart-project. Stage at D:/data/volumetric/mri/acdc (outside repo).

title: Apply largest-CC postprocessing in validate before Dice/EF (changes baseline numbers)
description:
Follow-up to cardiac-seg-52o (which lands keep_largest in cardioseg). 

We flagged it in the geometry review: predicted masks go straight to EF with NO postprocessing, so stray false-positive voxels inflate chamber volumes. Apply keep_largest (largest connected component, per class) to predictions in cardioseg/evaluation/validate.py before computing Dice + EF.

WHY separate from 52o: this CHANGES the reported baseline (likely improves EF, may nudge Dice), so it must be a deliberate, measured change — not bundled into a pure relocation.

DO:
- after predict_volume, clean each predicted class with keep_largest (and decide: largest-CC per foreground class, or per cavity only).
- re-run the seeded baseline (acdc + acdc_aug), record new metrics.json.
- update README Results numbers + note 'predictions cleaned with largest-CC' in the method line.
- likely shrinks the aug EF regression (3.1->4.2%) we saw — verify.

ACCEPTANCE: validate applies largest-CC; baseline re-run; README + metrics.json updated to the new numbers with the method noted; before/after EF MAE reported in the commit.

DEPENDS ON: cardiac-seg-52o (keep_largest must exist in cardioseg first).
notes: ABSORBS the keep_largest hoist. This issue is keep_largest's first real consumer (clean predictions before EF), so keep_largest ENTERS cardioseg HERE (typed, with a unit test) AND gets applied in validate — together, coupled to real usage. Decoupled from 52o: the util no longer rides in on the frame_indices dedup.

title: cardioview: dedup frame_indices -> use cardioseg.data.parse_info_cfg
description:
FOR: the cardioview agent. Reviewed by the cardioseg/learning agent.

Problem: cardioview re-implements geometry that should live in the cardioseg pipeline (the science layer), not the viz layer. The *better* mesher currently lives only in cardioview. Move it down; cardioview imports it.

MIGRATE from cardioview -> cardioseg:
1. chamber_surface() [cardioview/export_web.py] — marching cubes + Taubin smooth + decimate + largest-CC + isotropic resample + zero-pad edge-cap. The canonical chamber-mesh routine. Land in cardioseg (suggest: upgrade cardioseg/analysis/viz.py mesh_label, OR a new cardioseg geometry module). Type it against cardioseg/types.py (Volume [D,H,W], Spacing (z,y,x)).
2. keep_largest() [cardioview/geometry.py] — largest connected component. It's a SEGMENTATION post-process, belongs in cardioseg (not viz).
3. bbox_slices() [cardioview/geometry.py] — labeled-region bbox crop. General geometry util -> cardioseg.
4. frame_indices() [cardioview/export_web.py] duplicates cardioseg.data.parse_info_cfg — delete, use parse_info_cfg.

LEAVE in cardioview: pure rendering (pyvista Plotter, gltf export, web manifest), nearest_index (cine-specific), CHAMBERS palette.

ACCEPTANCE:
- cardioseg owns the mesh + geometry utils, typed (cardioseg/types.py vocabulary), with >=1 unit test (mesh a synthetic blob -> verts/faces/area sane; keep_largest drops a speck).
- cardioview imports them; render output unchanged (verify one render still works).
- Zero duplicated marching-cubes / largest-CC / bbox across the two folders.
- cardioseg tests stay green; NO change to EF/Dice numbers (this is a move, not a numerics change).

COORDINATION: stage explicit paths only (we have had cross-agent git entanglement); commit cardioseg + cardioview deliberately. pyvista + skimage already in cardioseg deps.

OUT OF SCOPE (separate follow-up issue): actually APPLYING keep_largest inside cardioseg.evaluation.validate to drop stray FP voxels — that CHANGES the baseline EF/Dice, so decide separately.
notes:
SCOPE NARROWED (cardioview-agent review, agreed). Separation of concerns: the VIEW owns display-mesh (Taubin smooth, decimate), the PIPELINE owns measurement-geometry. So:
- chamber_surface STAYS in cardioview by design — its smooth/decimate are display ops, not pipeline work. marching cubes already exists in cardioseg (analysis/viz.py mesh_label) => NOT a dup.
- keep_largest / bbox_slices: do NOT hoist speculatively (YAGNI; avoid cross-repo coupling for unused code). keep_largest moves only with its first consumer -> see cardiac-seg-w4l.
- bbox_slices: stays local in cardioview unless/until cardioseg actually crops-to-heart.
THIS ISSUE NOW = ONLY: dedup cardioview.frame_indices -> use cardioseg.data.parse_info_cfg. The single clean DRY win. Owner: cardioview agent (cardioseg already has parse_info_cfg; no cardioseg change needed).
close_reason: frame_indices now reuses cardioseg.data.parse_info_cfg; EF unchanged

title: cardioview-web: render chambers + EDV/ESV/EF readout
description: Phase 1: vtk.js page loads a patient glb (GLTFImporter), rotatable, dark theme like mri-sim. Side panel: EDV (ml full), ESV (ml empty), LVEF %, pred-vs-GT, held-out tag. Manifest JSON drives it.
close_reason: Web foundation shipped: rotatable chambers + EDV/ESV/EF panel, patient/phase, held-out tag, 5 tests + smoke

title: cardioview WEB viewer (TS + vtk.js) — epic
description: Browser heart viewer like mri-sim. Python precomputes inference->meshes->glb + EF manifest; web (TS+Vite+vtk.js) renders. Features: rotatable colored chambers, beating-cycle animation, EDV/ESV/EF readout, import nii.gz, patient picker.

title: cardioview: intensity volume raycast of ACDC hearts
description: New cardioview/ project: Python VTK/pyvista demo. First cut = true GPU volume raycasting of an ACDC patient MRI volume (greyscale, transfer function), spacing-aware (ACDC ~6-7x z-anisotropy). Loads via cardioseg.data.mri.data.load_ed_es/load_nifti. Later: seg overlay, EF validation, gallery over N hearts.
close_reason: Desktop pyvista volume raycast + seg overlay done; superseded by web viewer for the interactive demo

title: Precession froze during encode stages (warp sped the spins)
description: IDLE_WARP scaled the whole clock incl precession, so spins crawled during gz/gy/gx (no warp) and whipped during the warped idle - looked like they stopped in the encode stages. Decouple: precession on the un-warped speed clock (uniform across TR, pausable at speed 0); only cycleTime + T1 relax get the warp.
close_reason: Precession decoupled from warp; uniform across TR. 42 tests green

title: RF flip too light after realistic timing
description: TIP_DUR=0.15s was tuned for TR=2s era; with 18ms encode the flip only progressed ~12% during the watched window, finishing in the warped tail. Tie flip duration to the slice-select window so it completes fully before phase encode.
close_reason: Flip tied to slice-select window, full 90deg before phase encode; warp clamped to TR. 41 tests green

title: Time-warp the dead relaxation tail
description: Real TR is ~96% relaxation wait — boring. Fast-forward cycleTime 20x once past the readout window so encode plays real-time and the wait collapses. TR/TE labels stay real; seq caption marks the fast-forward.
close_reason: Idle tail warped 20x past readout; encode plays real-time. 40 tests green

title: Phase encoding actually twists spins (real Gy wind-up)
description: Gy was only coloring spins, not phase-encoding them. Add position-dependent azimuth twist to slab spins during the Gy window, steepness = peStep (the ky loop, steps each TR, resets at RF). Pure phaseEncodeOffset() in sequence.ts. Unit + integration tested.
close_reason: Gy now winds slab spins position-dependently, peStep steps the ky loop. 39 tests green

title: Realistic TR/TE (ms) + TE-scaled sequence timing
description: Replace 2s/0.5s with real T1w spin-echo values (TR 500ms, TE 15ms). Shared sequence.ts timing module scaled to TE so slice->phase->readout stays ordered for TE<<TR. Seq diagram zooms x-axis to encode window; ms labels. Speed slider max 1x (real time), min 0.01x slow-mo.
close_reason: Shipped: real TR/TE in ms, shared TE-scaled timing, zoomed seq diagram, speed max 1x. 34 tests + smoke green

title: Batch spin renderer into one polydata
description: Collapse ~756 per-spin actors (proton+line+cone x252) into one merged vtkPolyData with per-point RGBA scalars. One actor, one buffer upload per frame, GPU coloring. Scales to thousands of spins.
close_reason: Batched renderer shipped: 1 polydata, GPU scalar colors, grid 252->512, 31 tests + smoke green

title: Augmentation: close myo/RV Dice gap on ACDC
description: Baseline (no aug) Dice myo 0.822 / RV 0.859. Add train-time augmentation (flips, rotation, scale, intensity) to ACDCSliceDataset; retrain; compare vs baseline mean 0.871 / EF MAE 3.1%.

title: Refactor to good level: split validation out, hygiene, repro, tests
description: Quality pass before continuing. Move eval to evaluation/validate.py; per-patient spacing; seeds + checkpoint/metrics save; dataset unit tests; pyproject + README env note.

title: Role gap map: Xebia Senior AI Algorithm Developer (cardiac imaging)
description: Map each role requirement -> current repo evidence -> gap -> concrete action. Honesty-checked talking points for application/interview. Owner = audio/acoustic ML engineer ramping into medical imaging.
close_reason: Discussion only — user exploring; repo is public so no internal gap-map artifact committed

title: Computational-geometry thread (cross-cutting)
description: voxel->volume->EF, Simpson's method, marching cubes, wall thickness, resampling, ED/ES registration.

title: cardioview: decimate chamber meshes (glb ~5MB too big)
description: Marching-cubes meshes are ~5MB/glb. Decimate (quadric) + maybe draco compress so the web loads fast over many patients.

title: cardioview-web: patient picker / gallery over held-out set
description: Pick among the 20 held-out patients; gallery thumbnails, worst-EF-first. Drives the manifest.

title: cardioview-web: import nii.gz and render
description: Upload an .nii.gz in-browser, parse (nifti-reader-js), render the volume. Stretch: in-browser segmentation via ONNX (onnxruntime-web) -> chambers + EF on uploaded data.
close_reason: In-browser ONNX import works end-to-end; LV-cav volume matches pipeline. Single-volume scope; 4D/EF extension separate.

title: cardioview-web: beating-heart cycle animation
description: Segment all 4D cine phases offline (patient_4d.nii.gz), export per-phase chamber meshes, web loops them as a beating heart. Show cycle + note rhythm regularity. Play/pause/scrub.
close_reason: Beating-cycle animation shipped: 4D frames segmented offline, web loops with play/pause/scrub/ED-ES jump, 3-actor swap, legend

title: mri-sim: full 2D k-space acquisition (ky stepping)
description: k-space fills one line per TR but the phantom recon is effectively 1D-driven. Implement proper 2D acquisition: step ky across TRs, fill the 2D grid, reconstruct via 2D iFFT. Make the phase-encode wind-up steepness map exactly to the ky line being filled.

title: mri-sim: real ACDC slice as phantom (vtk reslice)
description: Replace the synthetic disk phantom with a real short-axis ACDC slice as the object. This is where vtk.js earns its keep (vtkImageData + reslice). Ties the visualizer to the actual cardiac-imaging data. Keep data outside the repo (gitignored, licensing).

title: mri-sim: T2* dephasing + FID/echo signal decay
description: Spins currently never lose coherence. Add per-spin off-resonance/inhomogeneity so the transverse spins fan out after excitation -> net signal (vector sum) decays = FID, recovering at the echo. Motivates WHY TE matters. Plot the net transverse magnitude as a signal trace.

title: mri-sim: split into its own repo
description: mri-sim lives in the cardiac-imaging repo for now. As it grows, split to a standalone repo (own README, deploy to GitHub Pages). Noted in SPEC/README.
close_reason: Not splitting; mri-sim stays in the cardiac-imaging repo

title: mri-sim: receive signal trace panel (FID/echo waveform)
description: Add a panel showing the measured signal over time within a TR: the FID after RF, decaying, then the gradient echo at TE during readout. Depends on T2* dephasing. Closes the loop spins -> measured signal -> k-space.

title: mri-sim: tissue contrast (T1/T2 weighting via TR/TE)
description: TR/TE currently only time the sequence. Give the phantom per-region T1/T2 so TR/TE choices change image CONTRAST (T1w vs T2w vs PD). Teaches the clinical knob: why you pick a sequence.

value: ACDC 2D U-Net baseline (40ep, no aug, 80/20 patient split): VAL Dice LV-cav 0.927, LV-myo 0.814, RV 0.843, mean 0.861; EF MAE 4.5%. Worst = HCM (EF d up to 16% - thick myo boundary). Run: pytorch_training_env python -m cardioseg.training.train --acdc. Near-SOTA on LV-cav; myo/RV have headroom (aug, more epochs).

ACDC label encoding VERIFIED on real masks (geometric myo-enclosure test): 0=bg, 1=RV cavity, 2=LV myocardium, 3=LV cavity. LV is label 3, NOT 1. synth.py + measure.py originally had lv_label=1 (would compute RV EF) — fixed to 3. Always disambiguate LV vs RV geometrically (src/data/mri/identify_lv_cavity), never trust a remembered int. Real ACDC: spacing z=10mm in-plane ~1.4-1.6mm, anisotropy 6-7x, intensity uncalibrated. DCM patients EF 10-29% (validates fix).

cardioview = Python VTK/pyvista 3D viz of ACDC + cardioseg results (new project, cardioview/). Run: PYTHONPATH=/d/personal_projects/cardiac-seg conda run -n pytorch_training_env python cardioview/render_volume.py --patient patient001 --phase ED. Needs PYTHONPATH=repo-root (script dir != root) + pyvista (installed in pytorch_training_env). Data: D:/data/raw/mri/acdc/{training,testing}/patientXXX (CARDIAC_DATA_ROOT). ACDC ~6-7x z-anisotropy -> resample isotropic. First cut = intensity raycast; next = seg overlay + chamber meshes + EF gallery (bd cardiac-seg-0tg).

cardioview/web = TS+Vite+vtk.js browser heart viewer. Canned: 4 held-out hearts (NOR/DCM/HCM/MINF) as beating glTF + EF panel, precompute via export_web.py (glb+manifest, gitignored). IMPORT: upload .nii.gz -> in-browser ONNX seg (onnxruntime-web, single-thread wasm, CDN) -> vtk marching-cubes -> render + chamber volumes. Model = bundled acdc_aug.onnx (export_onnx.py, 100% torch parity, gitignored under public/models) or import .onnx. Verified e2e: imported pt073 ED LV-cav=120mL == pipeline EDV. Pure modules tested: preprocess/chambers/select/util/metrics (24 web tests) + cardioview/tests geometry (7 py). Smokes: npm run smoke (canned), smoke:import (SCAN=path real nii.gz). Build target es2022 (top-level await). Single-volume import = no EF (needs ED+ES) - future.
